sections.title: Fats
sections.narration: 0.8g of fat. saturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, and omega3 are doing most of the work. for a legume, fibre and practicality are supposed to carry the case.
sections.subtitleText: 0.8g of fat. saturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, and omega3 are doing most of the work. for a legume, fibre and practicality are supposed to carry the case.

sections.title: Carbs
sections.narration: 60g of carbs. fibre is doing a lot of the work here. for legumes, the carbs look much better when fibre and protein are both backing them up.
sections.subtitleText: 60g of carbs. fibre is doing a lot of the work here. for legumes, the carbs look much better when fibre and protein are both backing them up.

sections.title: Proteins
sections.narration: 23.6g of protein. bioavailability and amino acid quality are both strong. protein support is a big part of what makes legumes worth it.
sections.subtitleText: 23.6g of protein. bioavailability and amino acid quality are both strong. protein support is a big part of what makes legumes worth it.

sections.title: Vitamins
sections.narration: everything else is kind of whatever.

sections.title: Minerals
sections.narration: magnesium at 35% daily value, iron at 29% daily value, potassium at 29% daily value stand out most. useful, but still not a major mineral edge.
sections.subtitleText: magnesium at 35% daily value, iron at 29% daily value, potassium at 29% daily value stand out most. useful, but still not a major mineral edge.

sections.title: Pros
sections.narration: on the plus side: one of the cheaper ways to buy useful nutrition. that matters for real diets, not just theory. does a lot of jobs at once. protein, fibre, and meal utility are why legumes stay valuable. filling for the calories. legumes usually earn a lot of practical value from satiety.

sections.title: Cons
sections.narration: now the downsides: texture is not universally loved. that can reduce how often people actually use them. can take planning or prep. that is a small but real friction cost. prep time can still be a barrier. dry legumes especially ask more from the cook.

closing.summary: Kidney Beans has a genuinely strong overall case. the practical downsides are real, but they do not outweigh the upside. best when you want fibre, protein, and actual meal utility together.
closing.useCaseNote: Best when you want fibre, protein, and actual meal utility together.
closing.cta: Would you rank it the same, or nah?

explanation.summary: Proteins are carrying this food most, while minerals are the biggest drag. It lands in S tier.
